devanagari: भक्तेर्महत्त्वं ध्यानयोगवर्णनं च । श्रीउद्धव उवाच । वदन्ति कृष्ण श्रेयांसि बहूनि ब्रह्मवादिनः । तेषां विकल्पप्राधान्यं उताहो एकमुख्यता ...
english_devnagari: bhaktermahattvaṃ dhyānayogavarṇanaṃ ca śrīuddhava uvāca vadanti kṛṣṇa śreyāṃsi bahūni brahmavādinaḥ teṣāṃ vikalpaprādhānyaṃ utāho ekamukhyatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation:
Uddhava said:
Expert expositors of Vedas state that there are many courses leading to the final emancipation of the Soul. Are they important in their own way depending on the adopter or is one of them prominent (in preference to the rest), O Kṛṣṇa?

devanagari: भवता उदाहृतः स्वामिन् भक्तियोगोऽनपेक्षितः । निरस्य सर्वतः सङ्गं येन त्वय्याविशेन्मनः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavatā udāhṛtaḥ svāmin bhaktiyogo nirasya sarvataḥ saṅgaṃ yena tvayyāviśenmanaḥ
translation: The path of Devotion which, is self-dependent (or disinterested) has been taught by you, O Lord. By following it, all attachment is warded off and the mind establishes itself in you.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । कालेन नष्टा प्रलये वाणीयं वेदसंज्ञिता । मयाऽदौ ब्रह्मणे प्रोक्ता धर्मो यस्यां मदात्मकः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca kālena naṣṭā pralaye vāṇīyaṃ vedasaṃjñitā mayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord replied:
In due course of time, this word of Mine which came to be designated as the Veda became extinct at the time of Deluge. At the beginning of Creation (of the universe), I communicated it to god Brahmā. It consisted of the dharma (the righteous duty) of devoting one’s mind to me exclusively.

devanagari: तेन प्रोक्ता स्वपुत्राय मनवे पूर्वजाय सा । ततो भृग्वादयोऽगृह्णन् सप्त ब्रह्ममहर्षयः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tena proktā svaputrāya manave pūrvajāya sā tato bhṛgvādayo
translation: The Lore was taught by Brahmā to his eldest son (Svāyambhuva) Manu. From him (Manu) the seven Brāhmaṇa sages, Bhṛgu and others, learnt it.

devanagari: तेभ्यः पितृभ्यः तत्पुत्रा देवदानवगुह्यकाः । मनुष्याः सिद्धगन्धर्वाः सविद्याधरचारणाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyaḥ pitṛbhyaḥ tatputrā devadānavaguhyakāḥ manuṣyāḥ siddhagandharvāḥ savidyādharacāraṇāḥ
translation: From those fathers (of creation) their progeny, gods, Dānavas (demons), Guhyaka‘s (Yakṣas) human beings, Siddhas Gandharvas, Cāraṇas, Kindevas (secondary deities), Kinnaras, Nāgas (serpents), Rākṣasas (Ogres), Kiṃpuruṣas (sub-human species with man-like appearance) and others learnt it. But being born with the dominance of different proportion of) attributes like Sattva, Rajas and Tamas they were of varied natural proclivities.

devanagari: किन्देवाः किन्नरा नागा रक्षः किम्पुरुषादयः । बह्व्यस्तेषां प्रकृतयो रजःसत्त्वतमोभुवः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kindevāḥ kinnarā nāgā rakṣaḥ kimpuruṣādayaḥ bahvyasteṣāṃ prakṛtayo rajaḥsattvatamobhuvaḥ
translation: From those fathers (of creation) their progeny, gods, Dānavas (demons), Guhyaka‘s (Yakṣas) human beings, Siddhas Gandharvas, Cāraṇas, Kindevas (secondary deities), Kinnaras, Nāgas (serpents), Rākṣasas (Ogres), Kiṃpuruṣas (sub-human species with man-like appearance) and others learnt it. But being born with the dominance of different proportion of) attributes like Sattva, Rajas and Tamas they were of varied natural proclivities.

devanagari: याभिर्भूतानि भिद्यन्ते भूतानां मतयस्तथा । यथाप्रकृति सर्वेषां चित्रा वाचः स्रवन्ति हि ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yābhirbhūtāni bhidyante bhūtānāṃ matayastathā yathāprakṛti sarveṣāṃ citrā vācaḥ sravanti hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sru class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sru class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sru class 1 verb]

translation: The natural tendencies which distinguish the different species from one another, differentiate their understanding as well. It is according to the nature of these that different and varied verbal expression (and interpretation of the Vedas) severally flow out.

devanagari: एवं प्रकृतिवैचित्र्याद् भिद्यन्ते मतयो नृणाम् । पारम्पर्येण केषाञ्चित् पाषण्डमतयोऽपरे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ prakṛtivaicitryād bhidyante matayo nṛṇām pāramparyeṇa keṣāñcit pāṣaṇḍamatayo
translation: It is in this manner that due to a variety in their natures, there is a diversity of understanding owing to hereditary and traditional interpretations (even though they themselves have not studied the Vedas) while others accept anti-Veḍic heretic views.

devanagari: मन्मायामोहितधियः पुरुषाः पुरुषर्षभ । श्रेयो वदंति अनेकांतं यथाकर्म यथारुचि ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: manmāyāmohitadhiyaḥ puruṣāḥ puruṣarṣabha śreyo vadaṃti anekāṃtaṃ yathākarma yathāruci

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śrā class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śrā class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ śrā class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ śrai class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Men whose minds are deluded by my Māyā enunciate various means of achieving the highest good according to their past acts (Karmas) and tastes.

devanagari: धर्ममेके यशश्चान्ये कामं सत्यं दमं शमम् । अन्ये वदंति स्वार्थं वा ऐश्वर्यं त्यागभोजनम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: dharmameke yaśaścānye kāmaṃ satyaṃ damaṃ śamam anye vadaṃti svārthaṃ vā aiśvaryaṃ tyāgabhojanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Some (i.e. Mīmāṃsakas) hold that performance of (sacrifices and other) acts prescribed in the Vedas leads to the summum bonum; others (viz. poets and writers on rhetorics) regard (good) renown as the highest blessing. Sages like Vātsāyana regard sexual enjoyment as the highest blessing; Yogīs advocate truthfulness, self-control and quiescence (as leading to the highest goal). Realists (like politicians) say that affluence, executive power as the highest purpose in life; some regard performance of sacrifices, worship of deities, austerities, gifts, selfdenial (like the vows yama and niyama) as the highest goal in life while materialists advocate liberality and enjoyment as the highest goal.

devanagari: केचिद् यज्ञतपो दानं व्रतानि नियमान् यमान् । आद्यंतवंत एवैषां लोकाः कर्मविनिर्मिताः । दुःखोदर्काः तमोनिष्ठाः क्षुद्रानंदाः शुचार्पिताः ...
english_devnagari: kecid yajñatapo dānaṃ vratāni niyamān yamān ādyaṃtavaṃta evaiṣāṃ lokāḥ karmavinirmitāḥ duḥkhodarkāḥ tamoniṣṭhāḥ kṣudrānaṃdāḥ śucārpitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ṛ ], [vocative plural from √ ṛ ]
[nominative plural from √ ṛ ], [vocative plural from √ ṛ ], [accusative plural from √ ṛ ]

translation: All these (above-mentioned) regions are the culminations of their acts and as such they have a beginning and an end (i.e. are temporary). They give rise to grief, end up in ignorance, afford trivial pleasures and are full of grief and anxiety (even while they are being enjoyed).

devanagari: मय्यर्पितात्मनः सभ्य निरपेक्षस्य सर्वतः । मयाऽत्मना सुखं यत्तत् कुतः स्याद् विषयात्मनाम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: mayyarpitātmanaḥ sabhya nirapekṣasya sarvataḥ mayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O gentle Uddhava! How can the (incomparable) happiness be enjoyed by people who have given themselves to sensual pleasure—the happiness which is shared with me by persons who have given up attachment to all things without expecting anything in return, and have dedicated their mind to me.

devanagari: अकिञ्चनस्य दान्तस्य शान्तस्य समचेतसः । मया सन्तुष्टमनसः सर्वाः सुखमया दिशः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: akiñcanasya dāntasya śāntasya samacetasaḥ mayā santuṣṭamanasaḥ sarvāḥ sukhamayā diśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ śam class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[genitive single from √ śam class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: All quarters are blissful unto him who owns nothing in this world, is self-controlled, serene in mind, regards all as equals and finds perfect delight in me.

devanagari: न पारमेष्ठ्यं न महेंद्रधिष्ण्यं । न सार्वभौमं न रसाधिपत्यम् । न योगसिद्धीः अपुनर्भवं वा । मय्यर्पितात्मेच्छति मद् विनान्यत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: na pārameṣṭhyaṃ na maheṃdradhiṣṇyaṃ na sārvabhaumaṃ na rasādhipatyam na yogasiddhīḥ apunarbhavaṃ vā mayyarpitātmecchati mad vinānyat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: He who has dedicated his heart to me does not hanker after the highest post (of god Brahmā) or the position of the rulership over gods or sovereignty over men or lordship over the subterranean regions or the mystic power of yoga or the Final Emancipation (Mokṣa) from which there is no return to Samṣāra. He wishes to have nothing else but me.

devanagari: न तथा मे प्रियतम आत्मयोनिर्न शङ्करः । न च सङ्कर्षणो न श्रीः नैवात्मा च यथा भवान् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: na tathā me priyatama ātmayonirna śaṅkaraḥ na ca saṅkarṣaṇo na śrīḥ naivātmā ca yathā bhavān
translation: Neither god Brahmā (who is born from me) nor Śaṅkara nor Saṅkarṣaṇa (my brother Balarāma) nor my Consort, goddess Śrī, nay not even my Soul is so dear to me as (a devotee like) you.

devanagari: निरपेक्षं मुनिं शान्तं निर्वैरं समदर्शनम् । अनुव्रजाम्यहं नित्यं पूयेयेत्यङ्‍घ्रिरेणुभिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: nirapekṣaṃ muniṃ śāntaṃ nirvairaṃ samadarśanam anuvrajāmyahaṃ nityaṃ pūyeyetyaṅ‍ghrireṇubhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: I always follow the foot-steps of a sage who expects nothing (in return for his penance or service), is tranquil, free from the feeling of enmity, looking all with an equal eye (regarding all impartially as equals), so that I can get myself[1] purified
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: So that I can purify all the worlds which abide in me.

devanagari: निष्किञ्चना मय्यनुरक्तचेतसः । शांता महांतोऽखिलजीववत्सलाः । कामैरनालब्धधियो जुषन्ति यत् । नैरपेक्ष्यं न विदुः सुखं मम ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: niṣkiñcanā mayyanuraktacetasaḥ śāṃtā mahāṃto kāmairanālabdhadhiyo juṣanti yat nairapekṣyaṃ na viduḥ sukhaṃ mama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Others cannot comprehend (much less realize), the immeasurable extent of the pure blissful state of mine which is unsullied by desires but which is enjoyed by the ego-less exalted souls who are perfect ‘have-nots’ (possessing nothing), and whose hearts are not attached to anything but are exclusively devoted to me; whose minds are tranquil, full of kindness to all beings, and whose hearts are untouched by desires.

devanagari: बाध्यमानोऽपि मद्‍भक्तो विषयैरजितेन्द्रियः । प्रायः प्रगल्भया भक्त्या विषयैर्नाभिभूयते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bādhyamāno prāyaḥ pragalbhayā bhaktyā viṣayairnābhibhūyate
translation: My devotee, who (in the primary stage), may not have control over his senses, will be disturbed by (the attraction of) the objects of senses, but is not generally overpowered by them due to his intense growing devotion.

devanagari: यथाग्निः सुसमृद्धार्चिः करोत्येधांसि भस्मसात् । तथा मद्विषया भक्तिः उद्धवैनांसि कृत्स्नशः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yathāgniḥ susamṛddhārciḥ karotyedhāṃsi bhasmasāt tathā madviṣayā bhaktiḥ uddhavaināṃsi kṛtsnaśaḥ
translation: Just as fire which is ablaze with flames reduces fuel to ashes, similarly devotion concentrated on me, burns down all the sins, O Uddhava.

devanagari: न साधयति मां योगो न साङ्ख्यं धर्म उद्धव । न स्वाध्यायः तपस्त्यागो यथा भक्तिर्ममोर्जिता ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: na sādhayati māṃ yogo na sāṅkhyaṃ dharma uddhava na svādhyāyaḥ tapastyāgo yathā bhaktirmamorjitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ūrj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ūrj class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ ūrj class 7 verb]

translation: Neither the path of Yoga nor that of Sāṅkhya or Dharma (righteous duties prescribed by Veda) or the study of the Vedas or performance of austerities or renunciation lead a person attain to me as does the intensive and well-advanced devotion.

devanagari: भक्त्याहमेकया ग्राह्यः श्रद्धयाऽत्मा प्रियः सताम् । भक्तिः पुनाति मन्निष्ठा श्वपाकान् अपि संभवात् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktyāhamekayā grāhyaḥ śraddhayā bhaktiḥ punāti manniṣṭhā śvapākān api saṃbhavāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [nominative single from √ grah ]

translation: I, the very beloved soul of saints, am realized through unswerving devotion and faith. Exclusive devotion established in me purifies even outcastes like the cāṇḍālas from their lowly birth.

devanagari: धर्मः सत्यदयोपेतो विद्या वा तपसान्विता । मद्‍भक्त्यापेतमात्मानं न सम्यक् प्रपुनाति हि ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmaḥ satyadayopeto vidyā vā tapasānvitā mad‍bhaktyāpetamātmānaṃ na samyak prapunāti hi
translation: Neither Dharma (righteousness even though) coupled with truthfulness and mercy nor knowledge reinforced by austerities can thoroughly purify of sins a person who is devoid of devotion.

devanagari: कथं विना रोमहर्षं द्रवता चेतसा विना । विनाऽनन्दाश्रुकलया शुध्येद् भक्त्या विनाऽशयः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ vinā romaharṣaṃ dravatā cetasā vinā vinā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: How can the heart be cleansed of impurities without the development of Devotion? And can intense devotion be indicated if the hairs do not stand (out of devotional love), and the heart melted with intense emotion (for god) and the tears of joy trickled down (the cheeks).

devanagari: वाग्गद्गदा द्रवते यस्य चित्तं । रुदत्यभीक्ष्णं हसति क्वचित्च । विलज्ज उद्गायति नृत्यते च । मद्‍भक्तियुक्तो भुवनं पुनाति ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: vāggadgadā dravate yasya cittaṃ rudatyabhīkṣṇaṃ hasati kvacitca vilajja udgāyati nṛtyate ca mad‍bhaktiyukto bhuvanaṃ punāti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ rud class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ rud class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ rud class 2 verb], [locative single from √ rud class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ rud class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ rud class 2 verb]

translation: He is really overwhelmed with intense devotion whose voice is choked with emotion and oral expression is faltering, whose heart melts (through his love for the Lord), who laments frequently (at his separation from the Lord) and laughs at time (with the sweet memory of the Lord’s mercy). Throwing away all bashfulness (or unmindful of social etiquettes), he sings loudly and dances—such a person endowed with my devotion purifies the world.

devanagari: यथाग्निना हेम मलं जहाति । ध्मातं पुनः स्वं भजते च रूपम् । आत्मा च कर्मानुशयं विधूय । मद्‍भक्तियोगेन भजत्यथो माम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: yathāgninā hema malaṃ jahāti dhmātaṃ punaḥ svaṃ bhajate ca rūpam ātmā ca karmānuśayaṃ vidhūya mad‍bhaktiyogena bhajatyatho mām
translation: Just as gold, when heated in fire, sheds off its dross and attains its pure state, so also the Soul rids himself of his impurities (and imprints of previous karmas) in heart through (intense) devotion to me and immediately gets absorbed in me. (lit. resorts to me).

devanagari: यथा यथाऽत्मा परिमृज्यतेऽसौ । मत्पुण्यगाथा श्रवणाभिधानैः । तथा तथा पश्यति वस्तु सूक्ष्मं । चक्षुर्यथैवाञ्जनसंप्रयुक्तम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā yathā matpuṇyagāthā śravaṇābhidhānaiḥ tathā tathā paśyati vastu sūkṣmaṃ cakṣuryathaivāñjanasaṃprayuktam
translation: The more the heart (of a person) gets purified by constantly) listening to and glorifying my sanctifying storties [stories?] (and repeating my name), the more it is able to visualize the subtle thing (viz. Paramātman), just as the eye can perceive minute objects (or underground treasures) when medicinal (or mystically charged) collyrium is applied to it

devanagari: विषयान् ध्यायतश्चित्तं विषयेषु विषज्जते । मां अनुस्मरतश्चित्तं मय्येव प्रविलीयते ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣayān dhyāyataścittaṃ viṣayeṣu viṣajjate māṃ anusmarataścittaṃ mayyeva pravilīyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: (Just as) the mind of a person constantly brooding over objects of senses gets attached to them, the mind of a person who is constantly meditating on me becomes absorbed in me.

devanagari: तस्मात् असदभिध्यानं यथा स्वप्नमनोरथम् । हित्वा मयि समाधत्स्व मनो मद्‍भावभावितम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt asadabhidhyānaṃ yathā svapnamanoratham hitvā mayi samādhatsva mano mad‍bhāvabhāvitam
translation: Therefore giving up all thoughts of unreal worthless worldly objects as imaginary things or cravings in a dream you should purify your mind with exclusive devotion unto me and firmly concentrate it on me.

devanagari: स्त्रीणां स्त्रीसङ्‍गिनां सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा दूरत आत्मवान् । क्षेमे विविक्त आसीनः चिन्तयेत् मां अतन्द्रितः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: strīṇāṃ strīsaṅ‍gināṃ saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dūrata ātmavān kṣeme vivikta āsīnaḥ cintayet māṃ atandritaḥ
translation: Avoiding from a distance association with women and those associated with women[2] (and those who cherish the misleading notion of one’s identity with the Lord), a person should subdue his mind and sit in a comfortable secure solitary place and contemplate on me shedding off all sluggishness.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
And works like Vātsyāyana’s Kāma-sūtra (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā)

devanagari: न तथास्य भवेत्क्लेशो बन्धश्चान्यप्रसङ्गतः । योषित् सङ्गात् यथा पुंसो यथा तत् सङ्गिसङ्गतः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: na tathāsya bhavetkleśo bandhaścānyaprasaṅgataḥ yoṣit saṅgāt yathā puṃso yathā tat saṅgisaṅgataḥ
translation: No such trouble or bondage would take place in association with others as a person would suffer in the company of women or those who are addicted to women.

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । यथा त्वां अरविन्दाक्ष यादृशं वा यदात्मकम् । ध्यायेत् मुमुक्षुः एतन्मे ध्यानं त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca yathā tvāṃ aravindākṣa yādṛśaṃ vā yadātmakam dhyāyet mumukṣuḥ etanme dhyānaṃ tvaṃ vaktumarhasi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Uddhava submitted:
May you be pleased, O Lord of Lotus-eyes to enlighten me on the methodology of meditation—in what manner, in what form and as what, a person desirous of Liberation should contemplate you.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । सम आसन आसीनः समकायो यथासुखम् । हस्तौ उत्सङ्ग आधाय स्वनासाग्रकृतेक्षणः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca sama āsana āsīnaḥ samakāyo yathāsukham hastau utsaṅga ādhāya svanāsāgrakṛtekṣaṇaḥ

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[present middle third single], [imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]

translation:
The Lord explained:
Seated on a level seat (which is neither too high nor too low and is covered with a soft blanket (keeping) one’s body erect, in a comfortable bodily posture (not necessarily a particular yogic posture like Paḍmāsana), placing the palms (open) on the laps and fixing one’s gaze on the tip of the nose (for mental concentration), one should cleanse the path of vital breath (the respiratory system) by the process of Prāṇāyāma—slow inhalation (with the left nostril) called Pūraka, retention of breath known as Kumbhaka and slow exhalation of Recaka (with the right nostril) and in the reverse order, one should slowly practice it (prāṇāyāma) retaining all the while control over the senses (technically known as pratyāhāra).

devanagari: प्राणस्य शोधयेत् मार्गं पूरकुम्भकरेचकैः । विपर्ययेणापि शनैः अभ्यसेत् निर्जितेन्द्रियः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇasya śodhayet mārgaṃ pūrakumbhakarecakaiḥ viparyayeṇāpi śanaiḥ abhyaset nirjitendriyaḥ
translation:
The Lord explained:
Seated on a level seat (which is neither too high nor too low and is covered with a soft blanket (keeping) one’s body erect, in a comfortable bodily posture (not necessarily a particular yogic posture like Paḍmāsana), placing the palms (open) on the laps and fixing one’s gaze on the tip of the nose (for mental concentration), one should cleanse the path of vital breath (the respiratory system) by the process of Prāṇāyāma—slow inhalation (with the left nostril) called Pūraka, retention of breath known as Kumbhaka and slow exhalation of Recaka (with the right nostril) and in the reverse order, one should slowly practice it (prāṇāyāma) retaining all the while control over the senses (technically known as pratyāhāra).

devanagari: हृद्यविच्छिन्नमोंकारं घण्टानादं विसोर्णवत् । प्राणेनउदीर्य तत्राथ पुनः संवेशयेत् स्वरम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: hṛdyavicchinnamoṃkāraṃ ghaṇṭānādaṃ visorṇavat prāṇenaudīrya tatrātha punaḥ saṃveśayet svaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ īr class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ īr ]
[vocative single from √ īr class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ īr ]

translation: One should propel with one’s breath the sacred sound Orii which, like a fine thread of lotus, is uninterruptedly jingling like a bell in the heart and then invest it with a nasal sound.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives another interpretation: The sound OM is continuous there from the Ādhāra cakra near the anus to the sahasrāra in the crown of the head. One should manifest it in the heart with one’s breath and invest it with a jingling bell-like sound and keep it (continuous). There have been different gradations of these anāhata sounds heard in this process, e.g. the Nādabindu Upaniṣad (32-35) states that the first sound is loud like the rumbling of clouds which goes on softening down to the jingling of bells:
śrūyate prathamābhyāse nādo nānāvidho mahān /
vardhamāne tathābhyāse śrūyate sūkṣma-sūkṣmata hi //
But the Haṃsopaniṣad gives the reverse order of loudness:
nādo daśavidho jāyate / cin iti prathamas' ciṇcīn iti dvitīyaḥ. / ghaṇṭānādo tṛtīyas...navamo bheri-nādaḥ, daśamo meghanādaḥ / navamam parityajya / daśamaṃ evābhyaset /
  —Upaniṣat-saṅgraha P. 147 (Motilal Banarsidass)

devanagari: एवं प्रणवसंयुक्तं प्राणमेव समभ्यसेत् । दशकृत्वस्त्रिषवणं मासाद्अर्वाग् जितानिलः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ praṇavasaṃyuktaṃ prāṇameva samabhyaset daśakṛtvastriṣavaṇaṃ māsādarvāg jitānilaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ ji class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ ji class 9 verb]

translation: In this way, he should invest his breath with Praṇava (Oṃ) and practise the breath-control ten times, at three times (morning, noon and the evening). Thus, he will attain control over his breath within a month.

devanagari: हृत्पुण्डरीकमन्तस्थं ऊर्ध्वनालमधोमुखम् । ध्यात्वोर्ध्वमुखमुन्निद्रं अष्टपत्रं सकर्णिकम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: hṛtpuṇḍarīkamantasthaṃ ūrdhvanālamadhomukham dhyātvordhvamukhamunnidraṃ aṣṭapatraṃ sakarṇikam
translation: He should contemplate that the heart that lies in the interior of his body like a lotus-bud drooping down with its stalk upwards (like the flower of a plantain tree) has blossomed with its eight petals and pericarp turned upwards and opened.

devanagari: कर्णिकायां न्यसेत् सूर्य सोमाग्नीन् उत्तरोत्तरम् । वह्निमध्ये स्मरेद् रूपं ममैतद् ध्यानमङ्गलम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: karṇikāyāṃ nyaset sūrya somāgnīn uttarottaram vahnimadhye smared rūpaṃ mamaitad dhyānamaṅgalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]
[vocative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: He should conceive that the son, the moon and the fire are placed one over the other in the pericarp (of that lotus). He should contemplate in that fire, my form so auspicious to meditate upon.

devanagari: समं प्रशान्तं सुमुखं दीर्घचारुचतुर्भुजम् । सुचारुसुन्दरग्रीवं सुकपोलं शुचिस्मितम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: samaṃ praśāntaṃ sumukhaṃ dīrghacārucaturbhujam sucārusundaragrīvaṃ sukapolaṃ śucismitam
translation: He should visualize my form as symmetrical, graciously gentle, with a charming countenance and four long beautiful arms, extremely graceful with a shapely neck, beautiful cheeks and bright smiles.

devanagari: समानकर्णविन्यस्तस्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् । हेमाम्बरं घनश्यामं श्रीवत्स श्रीनिकेतनम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: samānakarṇavinyastasphuranmakarakuṇḍalam hemāmbaraṃ ghanaśyāmaṃ śrīvatsa śrīniketanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphur class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sphur class 6 verb]

translation: (He should contemplate my form as having) dazzling alligator-shaped ear-rings beautifying my symmetrical ears, wearing garments of cloth of gold, in complexion dark-blue like clouds, and bearing on (the right and left side of my bosom the Śrī-vatsa mark and the abode of the goddess Śrī.

devanagari: शङ्खचक्रगदापद्म वनमालाविभूषितम् । नूपुरैः विलसत्पादं कौस्तुभप्रभया युतम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhacakragadāpadma vanamālāvibhūṣitam nūpuraiḥ vilasatpādaṃ kaustubhaprabhayā yutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ yu class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 3 verb]

translation: (He should conceive my form as) embellished with a conch (Pāñcajanya), a discus (Sudarśana) a mace (Kaumodakī) a lotus and a garland of forest-flowers (Vanamālā), with feet embellished with shining anklets and surrounded with halo of effulgent Kaustubha gem.

devanagari: द्युमत् किरीटकटक कटिसूत्राङ्गदायुतम् । सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरं हृद्यं प्रसाद सुमुखेक्षणम् । सुकुमारमभिध्यायेत् सर्वाङ्गेषु मनो दधत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: dyumat kirīṭakaṭaka kaṭisūtrāṅgadāyutam sarvāṅgasundaraṃ hṛdyaṃ prasāda sumukhekṣaṇam sukumāramabhidhyāyet sarvāṅgeṣu mano dadhat
translation: (He should meditate on my form as) adorned with a resplendent crown, bracelets, a girdle and armlets, beautiful in every part of the body, fascinating to the heart and with beautiful countenance and eye shedding grace (on the devotees) and very comely and delicate. One should concentrate one’s mind on all the parts of my form.

devanagari: इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यो मनसाऽकृष्य तन्मनः । बुद्ध्या सारथिना धीरः प्रणयेन् मयि सर्वतः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyo manasā buddhyā sārathinā dhīraḥ praṇayen mayi sarvataḥ
translation: Having withdrawn one’s senses from their objects, a wise person should, with the help of his intellect, the controller of the chariot in the form of one’s body, direct it, concentrating on me completely.

devanagari: तत्सर्वव्यापकं चित्तमाकृष्यैकत्र धारयेत् । नान्यानि चिन्तयेद् भूयः सुस्मितं भावयेत् मुखम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatsarvavyāpakaṃ cittamākṛṣyaikatra dhārayet nānyāni cintayed bhūyaḥ susmitaṃ bhāvayet mukham
translation: After withdrawing the mind so spread over the whole of my personality, one should (try to) concentrate on one limb only. He should now no more think of other limbs but should contemplate on my countenance beaming with smiles.

devanagari: तत्र लब्धपदं चित्तं आकृष्य व्योम्नि धारयेत् । तच्च त्यक्त्वा मदारोहो न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra labdhapadaṃ cittaṃ ākṛṣya vyomni dhārayet tacca tyaktvā madāroho na kiñcidapi cintayet
translation: Withdrawing his mind which is thus fixed up there (on my face), he should (now) concentrate it on the sky (i.e. my form which is the ultimate cause of all). (Lastly) diverting his mind from that, he should establish himself in me, the Absolute Brahman, and should not think of anything else (merging all the distinction between the subject, object and the process of thinking).

devanagari: एवं समाहितमतिः मां एवात्मानमात्मनि । विचष्टे मयि सर्वात्मन् ज्योतिर्ज्योतिषि संयुतम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ samāhitamatiḥ māṃ evātmānamātmani vicaṣṭe mayi sarvātman jyotirjyotiṣi saṃyutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: When one has thus established his mind (and intellect in me, he visualises me in his own self and his self in me the Universal Soul, just as (an individual) light is merged with the elemental light.

devanagari: ध्यानेनेत्थं सुतीव्रेण युञ्जतो योगिनो मनः । संयास्यत्याशु निर्वाणं द्रव्य ज्ञानक्रियाभ्रमः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: dhyānenetthaṃ sutīvreṇa yuñjato yogino manaḥ saṃyāsyatyāśu nirvāṇaṃ dravya jñānakriyābhramaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [ablative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [genitive single from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[ablative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [genitive single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation:
The mind of a yogi who concentrates his mind in this extremely intensive course of meditation, will, in no time, attain to the state of nirvāṇa (perfect serenity and bliss) and the misapprehension regarding substance, knowledge and action (or the triad of the Seer, Seeing and the Seen or the three types of miseries resulting from Fate, beings or forces of nature) completely disappears.
